checks.a_analysis: The output contains text that accurately reflects the source document. There are no instances of fabricated body text, introductions, or additional headings not present in the original document.

All contact information provided in the JSON, including the phone numbers (828-258-3939, 877-626-3198), address (160 Zillicoa Street), and meeting codes, matches the source document exactly. No fabricated contact information was found.

The output contains all major sections of the one-page source document (Header, Location, Remote Access, WebEx, Agenda). While a specific line is missing within the WebEx section, the section itself is present.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The WebEx section is missing the primary joining URL provided in the source. The source text contains four lines in this block (Header, URL, Meeting Number, Passcode), and the URL line is entirely omitted in the output. This represents a 25% loss of content for that section and a significant loss of functional utility.
checks.b_evidence_output: [The URL is missing from the elements between reading_order 5 and 7]

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Aside from the omitted URL, the text content accurately captures the wording and numeric values of the source. Minor reformatting such as the use of markdown-style asterisks for bolding is acceptable.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output and compared it to the source document. No segments of text or sections are duplicated in the output. Each element in the output corresponds to a unique section in the source document.
The output correctly identifies the document title as a Level 1 heading and the 'Agenda:' section as a Level 2 heading. There are no skipped levels or incorrect nestings.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table structural errors exist in the conversion.

The output maintains the logical reading order of the source document, proceeding from the header information through the contact details to the agenda list. While an element is missing (see S7), the relative sequence of the provided elements is correct.
The agenda items are correctly grouped into a single list structure. While the source does not use formal bullets, representing the scheduled items as a list is a faithful structural interpretation.

The source document contains a prominent WebEx URL link ('http://www.mymeetings.com/nc/join.php?sigKey=mymeetings&i=445085031&p=IndigoSnake&t=c') located after the 'WebEx:' label. This URL is completely missing from the output JSON, which jumps from reading_order 5 to 7, skipping the content that should be at index 6.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML output faithfully follows the source PDF. Both documents present the meeting information (Title, Date, Location, Remote Access details) followed by a chronological agenda list. Although the HTML uses responsive formatting (e.g., combining the location and phone number onto one line, and utilizing bullet points for the agenda), the information is presented in the same order and relative position. A user can easily navigate the information in the same way as they would in the original PDF.
The source PDF does not contain any tables; it consists entirely of structured text paragraphs and lists. Therefore, there are no tables to evaluate for rendering issues.
The source PDF is a text-only document and contains no meaningful images, logos, charts, or diagrams. No missing or broken images were identified in the HTML output.

There is no duplicated body content in the HTML output. The document title 'Federal Interagency Committee' appears at the top as a page header and again as a main section heading; however, this is an expected accessibility design pattern (navigation landmarks) and is explicitly excluded from being a defect. The actual body content (Date, Location, Agenda items) is not repeated.
